SVG fill-rule="evenodd" knockouts wrong on complex compound paths
notes: Pixi v8's SVGParser uses an area-sorted heuristic for evenodd compound paths (largest subpath = main shape, all others = cuts) at App/node_modules/pixi.js/lib/scene/graphics/shared/svg/SVGParser.mjs lines 88-99. For complex artwork (e.g. books.svg in our gene_key_icons set) the heuristic guesses wrong about which subpaths are holes, leaving inner regions filled that should be cut out. Browser rasterization respects evenodd correctly, which is why thumbnails look right but the canvas render does not.\n\nOptions: (a) per-asset opt-in fallback to raster mode for known-bad SVGs, (b) flatten compound paths to multiple separate paths with explicit fills before passing to Pixi, (c) ship a patched copy of Pixi's SVGParser. None are great. Punt until we have more affected assets to gauge severity.

name: Add cleaner to export features to remove all empty variables
notes:
How you would implement this
To do this safely without corrupting data, you shouldn't just drop all zeroes, because sometimes a user explicitly wants a property to be 0 when its default is 100 (like opacity).
Instead, you would write a small "cleaner" function right before generating the JSON payload:
    When saving, the function compares the layer's properties against DEFAULT_ANIMATABLES.
    Map over the data and
    delete any property key where currentValue === defaultValue.
    When loading the JSON back into the app, you simply run const mergedData = { ...DEFAULT_ANIMATABLES, ...savedData }.
Because we just set up fallback ?? 0 checks everywhere in the rendering logic (and established DEFAULT_ANIMATABLES in your store), your current codebase is practically ready to support this sparse data structure right now if you ever decide to write that cleanup function!

name: Change default thumbnail capture to 10s into animation instead of 1st frame
notes: Implemented: routed all 3 thumbnail-capture call sites (saveProject auto-snapshot, TopBar Set-thumbnail button, regenerateAllProjectThumbnails) through a new store action captureCurrentProjectThumbnail(). It seeks currentTime to min(10, project.duration), pauses if playing, awaits 2 RAFs so GeometryPlayer re-renders Pixi at the new time, extracts the canvas, then restores currentTime + isPlaying. Constant PROJECT_THUMBNAIL_CAPTURE_TIME at top of useStore.ts. Verify: short animation (<10s) captures at duration; long animation captures at 10s; live editing playhead is restored after auto-save snapshot; regenerate-all dashboard utility produces non-blank thumbs.

name: project thumbnail view
notes:
Implemented in two phases.

Phase 1 (commit 0aca4e4): saveProject snapshots the live Pixi canvas to a 320x180 PNG and uploads to v2-user-assets at {user_id}/_project-thumbnails/{id}.png. fetchProjects lists the prefix and signed-URLs the results into projectThumbnails. deleteProject best-effort removes the orphan.

Phase 2: Dashboard now has a list/grid view-mode toggle (top-right of the search box), a resizable divider between sidebar and preview, and defaults to grid mode with ~780px sidebar (list mode defaults to ~420px). View mode + sidebar width persist in localStorage. Grid mode shows projects as cards with thumbnail + name + last-edited; folders intentionally hidden in grid mode (manage them in list mode).

name: add URL in app for animations so you can "direct link" to any animation and preview it
notes: Implemented as ?p=<projectId> query param. App.tsx parses the URL on auth resolve and calls loadProject; a store subscription mirrors project.id back to the URL via replaceState whenever the editor opens / closes a project. TopBar > project dropdown gets a new 'Copy Link' item that puts the share URL on the clipboard. Open in editor view by default; preview-only / chrome-less view is a separate task if needed later.

name: there is a small bug, when I switch shape from SVG to polygon, it doesn't switch to polygon, it stays as SVG
notes: Fixed: type-switcher dropdown wasn't clearing customPath/customPaths from the layer config, so the polygon renderer's custom-shape branch kept drawing the SVG. Now clears them on switch.

name: fix SVGs
notes:
Fixed: SVG assets now render as vector via Pixi v8 GraphicsContext (parsed from source) instead of being rasterized through the SVG-to-canvas path. Compound paths + fill-rule="evenodd" knockouts survive intact. Recoloring still works — svgRecolor rewrites paint attrs in the source string before parsing into the context. PNG/JPEG assets keep the existing Sprite path. Also softened SVGO (disabled mergePaths + convertShapeToPath) so compound subpaths don't get flattened during upload optimization.

Files: App/src/rendering/AssetCache.ts (new getGraphicsContextSync + getMimeType, separate texture/context caches), App/src/rendering/GeometryRenderer.ts (SVG branch creates new Graphics(ctx) sized via getLocalBounds), App/src/utils/assetOptimizer.ts (SVGO overrides). Player bundle rebuilt.

Known limits: PixiJS v8 SVG parser doesn't support <clipPath>, <mask>, filters, gradient transforms, or <text>. Watch for assets using those — if any look wrong, file a follow-up.

name: fix color
notes:
Collapsed STROKE / FILL / GRADIENT into a single "Color" selector with a Flat | Gradient mode toggle. The single value writes to both strokeColor and fillColor in lockstep so primitives + SVG recoloring share one source of truth. Stroke is forced on whenever the unified UI writes (so a layer can never become invisible). The "Filled" toggle is now demoted to a small switch at the bottom of the Style menu — defaults off (rarely used).

Also fixed the "uploaded SVGs render with weird fills when stroke is on by default" bug: svgRecolor now walks with inherited paint context and only recolors paint regions that already exist on a node (or are inherited from an ancestor). SVG defaults — fill=black, stroke=none — mean stroke-only icons no longer pick up an unwanted fill, and fill-only icons no longer pick up an unwanted stroke.

Default new-layer color changed from #7a7a7a to #ffffff; fillColor now syncs to strokeColor at layer creation.

Files: App/src/components/Inspector.tsx (UI collapse), App/src/utils/svgRecolor.ts (inherited-paint walk), App/src/store/useStore.ts (new-layer defaults).

No data migration: old layers keep whatever stroke/fill/gradient values they had until edited. Editing any color in the new UI snaps strokeColor + fillColor to the same value.

Asset copy on project share / transfer
notes: Project rows reference asset IDs owned by the original user. Storage RLS enforces first path segment = auth.uid(), so the receiving user can't read the original blobs. When a project moves between users (share, duplicate-across-accounts, admin reassign), each referenced asset needs to be copied into the target user's space: new asset_folders / assets rows, new storage object at {new_user_id}/{new_asset_id}.{ext}, and every assetFolderId / assetId in the project's layers rewritten to point at the new ids. Consider an edge function that takes a project id + target user id and does the full clone in one shot.

name: Asset rebuild Stage H — Optional vector rendering for SVG assets
notes:
Branch feat/asset-vector-mode-stage-h. Spec in 2adf49a. Per-layer opt-in to true vector rendering for close-up / hero SVG layers where the 4x raster softens.

Config: vectorAsset?: boolean on LayerConfig (default false). Applies only when the resolved asset is image/svg+xml; raster mimes ignore the flag.

AssetCache: split the cache into two maps keyed on (assetId, mode) where mode in {texture, vector}. Add getGraphicsContextSync(assetId) that loads via Assets.load({ src, data: { parseAsGraphicsContext: true } }). invalidate(assetId) clears both modes. Keep getTextureSync unchanged so the default fast path is untouched.

Renderer: in the asset_set / asset_single branch, if effectiveConfig.vectorAsset && folderAssetEntry.mimeType === 'image/svg+xml', request the GraphicsContext and build new Graphics(ctx) instead of new Sprite(texture). Size via graphics.getLocalBounds() to compute the same targetMax / maxBound scale we apply to sprites. Fall back silently to the sprite path if the ctx is still loading.

Inspector: toggle next to the asset picker (Stage D UI). Only render the toggle when the current asset's mime is image/svg+xml. Label 'Crisp at any scale (vector)'. Helper text: 'Slower with many instances.'

Perf guardrail: soft-warn (small text under the toggle) when instances * symmetryMultiplier > 24. No hard block.

Migration: none needed — new optional field, default false.

Out of scope: per-instance mode switching, auto-promoting to vector at high zoom. Keep the flag static per layer.

name: Retire legacy astro/amino script bundles + toggles
notes: astro-data.js / amino-data.js (and the astro/amino export toggles) only feed legacy layer.type === "astrology" | "amino" projects. v2 migrates those to asset_set on load (useStore.ts:1182-1196), so for any project opened in v2 with seed folders set up, these bundles are unused. Auto-detect already returns false for migrated projects, and the toggles now hide entirely when not detected (done 2026-05-05).\n\nWhen retiring: (a) run a one-time scan — select id from projects where data::text like '%"type":"astrology"%' or data::text like '%"type":"amino"%' — to find any unmigrated stragglers and force-open them in v2 to migrate; (b) delete src/astro-entry.ts, src/amino-entry.ts, vite.astro.config.ts, vite.amino.config.ts, App/public/astro-data.js, App/public/amino-data.js, the build:astro/build:amino scripts in package.json; (c) drop usesAstro/usesAmino/includeAstro/includeAmino state and the gated toggle JSX from ExportModal.tsx + BatchExportModal.tsx; (d) drop the astro/amino branches from loadPlayerBundles in src/utils/exportHelpers.ts and the bundle paths in batchExport.ts. Coordinate with v1 retirement — v1 may still emit legacy layer types.
